2604.27981.title: ITS-Mina: A Harris Hawks Optimization-Based All-MLP Framework with Iterative Refinement and External Attention for Multivariate Time Series Forecasting
2604.27981.paper_summary: Multivariate time series forecasting plays a pivotal role in numerous real-world applications, including financial analysis, energy management, and traffic planning. While Transformer-based architectures have gained popularity for this task, recent studies reveal that simpler MLP-based models can achieve competitive or superior performance with significantly reduced computational cost. In this paper, we propose ITS-Mina, a novel all-MLP framework for multivariate time series forecasting that integrates three key innovations: (1) an iterative refinement mechanism that progressively enhances temporal representations by repeatedly applying a shared-parameter residual mixer stack, effectively deepening the model's computational capacity without multiplying the number of distinct parameters; (2) an external attention module that replaces traditional self-attention with learnable memory units, capturing cross-sample global dependencies at linear computational complexity; and (3) a Harris Hawks Optimization (HHO) algorithm for automatic dropout rate tuning, enabling adaptive regularization tailored to each dataset. Extensive experiments on six widely-used benchmark datasets demonstrate that ITS-Mina achieves state-of-the-art or highly competitive performance compared to eleven baseline models across multiple forecasting horizons.
2604.27981.paper_summary_zh: 多變量時間序列預測在許多現實應用中扮演著關鍵角色，包括金融分析、能源管理和交通規劃。<lb/>雖然基於Transformer的架構在這項任務中已獲得普及，但最近的研究顯示，較簡單的基於MLP的模型可以以顯著降低的計算成本達到具有競爭力或更優的性能。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了ITS-Mina，一個新穎的全MLP框架，用於多變量時間序列預測，該框架整合了三項關鍵創新：<lb/>(1) 一種迭代精煉機制，通過重複應用共享參數的殘差混合器堆疊，逐步增強時間表示，有效地加深模型的計算能力，而不增加不同參數的數量；<lb/>(2) 一個外部注意模塊，用可學習的記憶單元取代傳統的自注意，捕捉跨樣本的全局依賴，計算複雜度為線性；<lb/>(3) 一種哈里斯老鷹優化（HHO）算法，用於自動調整丟棄率，實現針對每個數據集的自適應正則化。<lb/>在六個廣泛使用的基準數據集上進行的廣泛實驗表明，ITS-Mina在多個預測範疇中相比於十一個基準模型達到了最先進或高度競爭的性能。
2604.27981.author: Pourya Zamanvaziri et.al.
2604.27981.authors: Pourya Zamanvaziri, Amirhossein Sadr, Aida Pakniyat, Dara Rahmati
